_description: Per-layer phi (φ) calibration offsets for sim-to-real correction. Applied as: metric_corrected = metric_sim + phi_offset. All zeros = simulation only. Place actual metric corrections under 'offsets' sub-dict.

L._calibration_formula: In-rich a-IGZO (champion cation In~0.6); anchored vs 1:1:1
_sources: Nomura & Hosono et al. 2004 (Nature 432, 488) / 2010 (STAM 11 044305): 1:1:1 anchor, Hall mu ~10-16 cm2/Vs
a-IGZO/In-rich heterostructure 2025 (J. Alloys Compd. S0925838825035960): In-rich channel mu=42.1 cm2/Vs, on/off 4.8e8
In-rich consensus: mu rises monotonically with In/(In+Ga) (In-5s overlap); ~40-50 cm2/Vs at cation In 0.45-0.6, spread ~+-20% (process-fixed)
Tradeoff (Sci. Rep. 2024 s41598-024-81556-y): In-rich -> negative Vth, higher Ioff, worse NBIS; amorphous-stable window tops out ~cation In 0.5-0.7
L._correction_note: AUDIT/RELIABILITY follow-up 2026-06-03: L's +-40% was the stack's fab-failure bottleneck. A focused review of the In-rich REGION (where the champion sits, cation In~0.6) shows it is far better constrained than the full composition range: independent groups agree to ~+-20% (process-fixed), so +-40% was too pessimistic. Tightened to 28% (covers +-20% physics + deposition-method scatter). The model still UNDER-predicts In-rich mobility (~20 vs lit ~40-48) because the percolation/IIS penalties (audit-verified physics) are conservative at high In -- this under-estimate is SAFE (real device better than modelled) and is left as a documented limitation; a future model revision could steepen the In-coop / ease the In-rich percolation penalty rather than inflate the additive offset (which would overshoot low-In, the EM lesson).
L._notes: mobility offset 2.0 -> 4.0 (modest, stays conservative everywhere: 1:1:1 ~9.7 vs lit ~13; champion ~22 vs lit ~45 -- both still under-estimates, no overshoot). SS offset 20 (model interface-trap term carries the rest). crystallization_risk uncalibrated -> 0. Update with in-house Hall mobility + NBIS runs at the In-rich champion composition.

_sources: Delaney et al. 2020 (Adv. Funct. Mater. 10.1002/adfm.202002447) & 2021 (Sci. Adv. 7 eabg3500, PMC8208718): n_amorph=3.285, n_cryst=4.050, delta_n=0.765 at 1550 nm (same Southampton lineage; anchor)
Opt. Mater. Express 12(7):2815 (2022): independent group, evaporated Sb-Se, delta_n up to 0.823 at ~1550 nm
Adv. Phys. Res. review 2024 (apxr.202400080): n_amorph=3.27, n_cryst=4.04, delta_n~0.77 at 1550 nm
Composition trend (Zhang 2019 Nat. Commun. 10:4279; arXiv:2510.14990): Ge (GSSe) and Si incorporation LOWER delta_n vs pure Sb2Se3; Te (GSST) raises it; Cl has no telecom index effect
Loss: k < 1e-5 in both phases at 1550 nm across all photonics sources (at/below ellipsometry floor; device loss ~100 dB/cm crystalline)
PM._notes: Web-literature recalibration 2026-06-03 (was single-point +-50%). Pure Sb2Se3 delta_n is well-constrained: 0.77 +- 0.06 across multiple groups -> uncertainty tightened 50% -> 18%. The structural underprediction remains (Clausius-Mossotti element mixing gives raw n_amorph~2.71/n_cryst~3.13 vs real 3.285/4.05; +0.343 offset anchors pure-Sb2Se3 delta_n to 0.765). PAIRED MODEL FIX (PM/api.py, same date): the glass-former GLASS_DELTA_N_BOOST had the WRONG SIGN (+0.12, raising delta_n for Ge); literature shows Ge/Si LOWER delta_n (GSSe << Sb2Se3), corrected to -0.05. Residual 18% (vs pure-material 8%) reflects: no direct 1550nm n/k for Sb2Se3:Ge:Cl specifically (only Ge/Si analogs), and k unresolved (upper bound only). Update when ellipsometry of the actual Ge:Cl champion film is available.
PM._excluded_correction: loss_k: literature says k < 1e-5 (detection floor) for pure Sb2Se3; champion Ge+Cl dopants may raise k; sputtered film roughness also contributes; no reliable literature value for champion formula.

_sources: Muller et al. 2012 (Nano Lett. 12, 4318, 10.1021/nl302049k): ~9 nm (sub-10 nm) Hf0.5Zr0.5O2 TiN/HZO/TiN, Pr~17 uC/cm2 (2Pr~34), k~30
Flexible/ultrathin HZO 2023-2024 (ScienceDirect/Springer): 10 nm 2Pr 28-34 uC/cm2, endurance 1e10-1e11 cycles
TiN/HZO/TiN wake-up (PMC11789571 / PMC10224430): 2Pr 12->28 uC/cm2 after cycling (wake-up not modelled, folded into uncertainty)
E._notes: Web-literature check 2026-06-03 found the sim is ACCURATE at the canonical anchor: predicts Pr=16.1 uC/cm2 (lit ~16-17), k=27.4 (lit ~27-32), endurance=3.3e10 (lit 1e10-1e11). Promoted from 'uncalibrated' -> 'literature_calibrated'; only small +offsets needed. Residual uncertainty 25% dominated by wake-up cycling (+20-40% Pr, not in model) and electrode/thickness variation. endurance left at offset 0 (additive correction ill-posed across orders of magnitude; sim already in range). Update with in-house P-E + C-V loops when available.

_sources: PMC9030765: x=0.20, d33=9.9 pC/N (pulsed DC magnetron, Si/Mo, 300C)
PMC8585448: x=0.29, d33=12.6 pC/N (pulsed DC magnetron, SOI, 300C)
Akiyama et al. 2009 (Adv. Mater. 21:593): x=0.43, d33=27.6 pC/N (dual reactive cosputter)
Mertin/Zywitzki et al. 2017 (Surf. Coat. Technol. S0257897216312129): x~0.4, d33=26.9-27.3 pC/N (standard reactive magnetron)
Fichtner/Wingqvist consensus: d33 peak ~26-28 pC/N near x=0.40-0.43 before the wurtzite->rocksalt instability
EM._correction_note: AUDIT M-6/M-7 (2026-06-03): the prior calibration anchored to a LOW cluster (9.9/12.6/19.2) and EXCLUDED Akiyama 27.6 on a FALSE 'different technique' rationale — standard reactive magnetron (Mertin 2017) independently reaches 26.9-27.3, so the true champion-region bias was ~-30 to -37%, not -8.9%. FIXED primarily in the model: EM/api.py D33_A1/A2 rescaled x1.5 so d33_bulk(0.40)=25.1 (eff ~23) matches the ~26-27 consensus. This offset is now only a small residual; the false _excluded note is removed.
EM._notes: After the model rescale, residual model bias is small; phi=+1.5 pC/N (kept small deliberately so it does not overshoot the low-x end the way a larger additive offset would — the rescale carries the magnitude, the offset only trims the champion region; champion x~0.36 -> d33 ~21). Uncertainty 25% spans technique scatter (pulsed-DC vs reactive-magnetron) and the texture factor. Update with 5+ measurements from the actual deposition system.
